events.title: Diesel spill from barge T/B Annahootz into Cook Inlet at Port of Anchorage
events.description: NOAA IncidentNews reports that the barge T/B Annahootz spilled about 500 gallons of diesel while loading at the Port of Anchorage, releasing a sheen into Cook Inlet. Cleanup activities recovered an oil-water mixture.

sources.headline: Diesel spill from barge T/B Annahootz into Cook Inlet at Port of Anchorage

key_quotes.quote: spilled approximately 500 gallons of diesel into Cook Inlet while loading at the Port of Anchorage

events.title: Oil sheen in the Neuse River traced to grounded tug Snapper
events.description: NOAA IncidentNews reports that a six-mile oil sheen was observed on the Neuse River near Scott's Creek and that investigators determined the source was the grounded tug Snapper, which rolled over and released diesel.

location.name: Neuse River near Scott's Creek, New Bern, North Carolina, USA

sources.headline: Oil sheen in the Neuse River traced to grounded tug Snapper

key_quotes.quote: a six-mile oil sheen was reported in the Neuse River

events.title: Waste oil slick from unnamed outfall into Colgate Creek (Dundalk)
events.description: NOAA IncidentNews reports that an oil slick was observed entering Colgate Creek from an unnamed outfall/tributary in Dundalk, Maryland. The product of concern was described as waste oil.

sources.headline: Waste oil slick from unnamed outfall into Colgate Creek (Dundalk)

key_quotes.quote: an oil slick from an unnamed outfall tributary leading to Colgate Creek was reported

events.title: Dredge Sugar Island discharged No. 2 fuel oil into the East River
events.description: NOAA IncidentNews reports that the dredge Sugar Island discharged nearly 8,000 gallons of No. 2 fuel oil during an internal fuel transfer when a tank was overfilled. The oil spread as sheen along the East River.

location.name: East River, New York, USA (between Governors Island and Roosevelt Island)

sources.headline: Dredge Sugar Island discharged No. 2 fuel oil into the East River

key_quotes.quote: discharged nearly 8,000 gallons of No. 2 fuel oil during an internal fuel transfer

events.title: Mystery spill in Oriental Harbor traced to Fulchers Point Pride Seafood fuel tanks
events.description: NOAA IncidentNews reports that a thick oil slick was observed in Oriental Harbor, a tributary of the Neuse River. The investigation attributed the source to fuel tanks at Fulchers Point Pride Seafood.

location.name: Oriental Harbor (tributary of the Neuse River), North Carolina, USA

sources.headline: Mystery spill in Oriental Harbor traced to Fulchers Point Pride Seafood fuel tanks

key_quotes.quote: a thick oil slick was reported in Oriental Harbor

events.title: Tropical Storm Debby causes heavy rain, flooding, and landslides in the Windward Islands (notably Saint Lucia)
events.description: Sources describe Tropical Storm Debby affecting parts of the Lesser Antilles/Windward Islands in September 1994, bringing heavy rainfall and damaging impacts. Reported impacts include flooding and landslides, especially noted for Saint Lucia.

events.title: Tropical Depression Ten brings heavy rainfall to western Cuba and the Florida Keys
events.description: NOAA/WPC documentation for Tropical Depression Ten (1994) indicates the system formed near the northwest Caribbean and produced heavy rainfall. Reported rainfall impacts include western Cuba and the Florida Keys before the system weakened/merged.

location.name: Havana, Cuba (proxy for western Cuba impacts)

events.title: Severe drought conditions reported across eastern and northern Australia (September 1994)
events.description: An Australian disaster/knowledge-base entry describes severe drought affecting eastern and northern Australia during 1994, with conditions present by September. The entry references widespread impacts consistent with major rainfall deficits across affected regions.

sources.source_name: Australian Institute for Disaster Resilience (AIDR) Knowledge Hub
sources.headline: Severe drought in eastern and northern Australia (1994)

events.title: Near-record warmth reported in eastern Europe and southwestern Russia (September 1994)
events.description: A NOAA CPC climate assessment for 1994 reports that areas of near-record warmth shifted into eastern Europe and southwestern Russia during September 1994. The assessment situates this within broader late-1994 warmth patterns over Europe.

location.name: Russia (proxy for southwestern Russia referenced in assessment)

events.title: Offshore Northern California earthquake near Ferndale, California
events.description: An offshore earthquake occurred west of Ferndale, California in the Cape Mendocino region. A USGS ShakeMap metadata page for event nc21159189 lists the location as approximately 88.3 km W of Ferndale, CA.

location.name: Offshore Northern California (~88 km W of Ferndale, California, USA)

events.title: Earthquake southeast of South Lake Tahoe near the California–Nevada border
events.description: A local magnitude ~6.1 earthquake occurred near the California–Nevada border, about 32 km southeast of South Lake Tahoe. California Geological Survey material summarizes strong-motion recordings from stations that captured this event.

location.name: Near CA–NV border (~32 km SE of South Lake Tahoe)

events.title: Freezeout Fire listed in NPS Morning Report large-fire summary (Sept 1994)
events.description: The NPS Morning Report large-fire summary (dated 1994-09-06) listed the Freezeout Fire in the Wallowa-Whitman National Forest. The report noted acreage figures and containment status, with an estimated containment date of 1994-09-07.

key_quotes.quote: Large-fire summary ... Freezeout ... Wallowa-Whitman NF ... acreage and containment information

events.title: Twin Lakes Fire listed in NPS Morning Report large-fire summary (Sept 1994)
events.description: The NPS Morning Report large-fire summary (dated 1994-09-06) listed the Twin Lakes Fire in the Wallowa-Whitman National Forest. The report noted acreage figures and containment status, with an estimated containment date of 1994-09-10.

key_quotes.quote: Large-fire summary ... Twin Lakes ... Wallowa-Whitman NF ... acreage and containment information

events.title: Idaho City Complex listed in NPS Morning Report large-fire summary (Sept 1994)
events.description: The NPS Morning Report large-fire summary (dated 1994-09-06) listed the Idaho City Complex in the Boise National Forest. The report noted acreage figures and containment status, with an estimated containment date of 1994-09-07.

key_quotes.quote: Large-fire summary ... Idaho City Complex ... Boise NF ... acreage and containment information

events.title: Howling prescribed natural fire reported in NPS Morning Report (Glacier NP, Sept 1994)
events.description: The NPS Morning Report dated 1994-09-06 noted the Howling prescribed natural fire in Glacier National Park, reporting that it had burned about 375 acres as of the referenced Friday.

key_quotes.quote: Howling prescribed natural fire ... burned about 375 acres

events.title: East Coast Queensland bushfires: major outbreaks reported 27–29 Sept 1994
events.description: AIDR’s hazard resource on the East Coast Queensland bushfires describes major outbreaks on 27–29 September 1994 affecting multiple areas in Queensland, including Beerburrum State Forest. Reported impacts included evacuations, home losses, injuries to volunteer firefighters, and plantation losses.

sources.source_name: Australian Institute for Disaster Resilience (AIDR) Knowledge Hub
sources.headline: Bushfire - East Coast Queensland

events.title: USFWS lists three vernal pool fairy shrimp as endangered and vernal pool tadpole shrimp as threatened
events.description: A U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service final rule listed the Conservancy fairy shrimp, longhorn fairy shrimp, and vernal pool fairy shrimp as endangered, and listed the vernal pool tadpole shrimp as threatened. The rule cited extensive historical loss and ongoing modification or destruction of California vernal pool habitats affecting these endemic crustaceans.

sources.headline: Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Determination of Endangered Status for Three Vernal Pool Fairy Shrimp and Threatened Status for the Vernal Pool Tadpole Shrimp

key_quotes.quote: determination of endangered status for three vernal pool fairy shrimp and threatened status for the vernal pool tadpole shrimp

events.title: EPA issues immediate final rule incorporating Minnesota’s approved hazardous waste program under RCRA (40 CFR Part 272)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published an immediate final rule to codify Minnesota’s authorized hazardous waste management program under RCRA by incorporating state statutes and regulations by reference into 40 CFR Part 272. The action clarifies the scope of the federally enforceable hazardous waste program for Minnesota.

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA Federal Register RCRA Part 272 Minnesota 1994-09-06

events.title: EPA promulgates final Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) Phase II rule establishing Universal Treatment Standards (UTS)
events.description: EPA published a final rule under RCRA’s Land Disposal Restrictions program setting treatment standards for additional organic toxicity characteristic wastes and newly listed wastes. The rule also establishes Universal Treatment Standards intended to standardize and simplify LDR treatment requirements.

sources.headline: List of Federal Register notices pertaining to hazardous waste

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA Federal Register LDR Phase II UTS 1994-09-19

events.title: EPA proposes air standards to reduce pollutants from municipal waste combustors (MWC) nationwide
events.description: EPA announced a proposed action to strengthen and expand air pollution standards for municipal waste combustors. The proposal targets reductions in pollutants such as dioxins and certain metals, under Clean Air Act authorities.

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA proposal municipal waste combustors air standards 1994-09-01

events.title: EPA proposes requirements for lead-based paint activities (TSCA Title IV) including certification and state program authorization framework
events.description: EPA issued a proposed rule describing requirements for lead-based paint activities, including certification provisions and an approach for authorizing state programs. The proposal is intended to regulate lead-based paint hazard reduction activities.

sources.headline: Proposed rule: Requirements for lead-based paint activities (September 2, 1994)

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA TSCA Title IV proposed rule lead-based paint activities 1994-09-02
